content: Sam, 31, backend/ML engineer at a Paris logistics startup. Date 2026-06-13. Revision of plan from 2026-03-01: keeping Valencia but dropping the race goal from 3h45 to 4h00 because of the knee. Concretely: I accept the slower target — 4:00 -> that's 240 minutes total, about 5:41/km average instead of ~5:20/km for 3:45. Thinking out loud: less stress on knee, lower training intensity, keep race in Valencia, adjust marathon-pace work, and protect the base. Next steps: decide how much to cut weekly load, how to structure long runs, and what to tell Marc (coach).
How long have you had the knee issue and what are your current longest run and weekly mileage?

Knee pain started late April (roughly 2026-04-28), persistent patellofemoral-type pain during/after runs. Longest run was 28 km on 2026-06-06. Weekly mileage right now ~50 km. I've seen physio twice since 2026-05-10, doing icing and basic rehab. That's why I'm dropping 3h45 -> 4h00.

Okay — switching target to 4:00 lowers required pace to ~5:41/km. Suggest cutting weekly volume ~10–15% and keeping long runs 28–30 km with more easy miles; schedule a physio check in two weeks (2026-06-27). Do you want a 12-week adjusted plan toward 4:00?

Yes — a 12-week plan starting 2026-06-15. Concrete preferences: reduce weekly to 42–45 km, replace one run per week with pool/cross-train, intervals at lower intensity (e.g. 5x1 km at comfortable threshold, not full VO2), marathon-pace rehearsals at 5:41/km for 10–18 km, long runs 26–30 km but no back-to-back hard days, physio every 2 weeks, and keep icing/strength work. I will update Strava and tell Marc. Confidence ~6/10. Which metric should I use to judge sessions now — pace, HR, or running power?
Use running power + RPE as primary; pace is useful on flat sections, HR lags with intensity. Shall I draft the message to Marc and the Strava/calendar updates?

Coach is Marc. Please refine this draft into a concise message I can send: "Hi Marc — quick revision of my Valencia plan: keep the race in Valencia but drop my target from 3h45 to 4h00 because of ongoing knee issues. Start adjusted plan 2026-06-15: reduce weekly from 50 to 42–45 km, long runs 28–30 km, add 1 pool/cross session per week, physio every 2 weeks, and use running power + RPE for session targets. Confidence ~6/10. Can you update my plan and the training calendar?" Make it shorter and direct.

Refined two-line message: "Hi Marc — keeping Valencia but lowering my goal from 3:45 to 4:00 due to knee issues. From 2026-06-15 please reduce my weekly to 42–45 km, long runs 28–30 km, add 1 pool session/week, physio q2w, and use running power + RPE for targets — confidence ~6/10. Can you update the plan and calendar?" Send as-is?

Yes — send that. One tweak: add "please keep me informed of changes to key sessions" and a calendar note to check the knee two days after every long run. Also prepare a short Strava update: something like "Valencia: keeping the race, switching target to 4:00 to manage a knee issue. Training adjusted from 2026-06-15." Add that as the Strava text and set goal to 4:00. Confirm you add the physio reminders and the knee-check reminders.

Final Marc message with tweak: "Hi Marc — keeping Valencia but lowering my goal from 3:45 to 4:00 due to knee issues. From 2026-06-15 please reduce weekly to 42–45 km, long runs 28–30 km, add 1 pool session/week, physio q2w, use running power + RPE, and please keep me informed of changes to key sessions — confidence ~6/10. Can you update the plan and calendar?" Strava text: "Valencia: keeping the race, switching target to 4:00 to manage a knee issue. Training adjusted from 2026-06-15." I will add the 2-day-after-long-run knee check reminders and physio reminders to the calendar. Done?
